data.title: Stock futures drop following financial stock declines, with banking shares falling due to bad loans and fraud allegations. S&P 500 down 0.29%, Nasdaq 100 down 0.45%. CSX Corp rose by 3.45% in premarket on Friday, surpassing analyst estimates with revenue of $3.59 billion and adjusted earnings of 44 cents per share. Fed expected to cut interest rates in October meeting. 100% chance as per CME Group's FedWatch tool. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: U.S. stock futures remained lower following losses on Thursday, particularly in banking and financial sectors due to loan-related issues with Zions Bancorporation and fraud allegations on Western Alliance Bancorp. JPMorgan Chase CEO's cautionary remarks after Tricolor Holdings' bankruptcy added to concerns, with the 10-year Treasury bond at 3.95% and two-year bond at 3.38%. Markets predict a 100% likelihood of a Federal Reserve rate cut in October. Various ETFs tracking major indexes like S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 dropped, with the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust at $660.59 (-0.0076%) and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF at $599.17 (-0.14%). CSX Corp. recorded a 3.45% rise in premarket, exceeding Q3 revenue and earnings estimates, maintaining a solid growth trend.

data.title: Swiss economic growth to slow due to US tariffs; GDP expected at 0.9% in 2026, lowest since 2020. US imposed tariffs at 31% in April and increased to 39% on almost $61 billion in goods. Top exports include chemicals, pharmaceuticals, machines, electronics, watches, precision instruments. Swiss companies affected include Swatch Group AG, Compagnie Financière Richemont SA, Logitech International SA, Nestlé SA, and pharmaceuticals subject to 100% tariffs unless produced in the US. US represents 18% of Swatch's sales in 2024.
data.description: The Swiss economy is expected to slow down in 2026, after a year of below-average performance and projected growth of 1.3% in 2025, which is lower than usual. The government forecasts a further decrease to 0.9% in 2026, the lowest since a 2.4% contraction in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic. Uncertainty in international trade and economic policies, particularly with higher US tariffs, is affecting the economic outlook. US President Donald J. Trump announced tariffs of 31% for Switzerland in April, later increasing it to 39% on $61 billion in Swiss exports to the US, impacting sectors like chemicals, pharmaceuticals, machines, electronics, watches, and precision instruments. Swiss companies facing 100% tariffs for branded pharmaceutical products in the US unless they have production facilities there include Swatch Group AG, Compagnie Financière Richemont SA, Logitech International SA, and Nestlé SA. For instance, Swatch reported that the US accounted for 18% of its global sales in 2024. This data is from CNBC and Benzinga.com.

data.title: PNK Group starts building a 268,000 sq. ft. multi-functional facility in Southern New Jersey. Ceremony on Oct. 14 in Camden County.
data.description: In southern New Jersey, the industrial developer PNK Group commenced construction on a 268,000-square-foot versatile facility. The groundbreaking ceremony for the PNK Waterford project was held on October 14 in Camden County.

data.title: Pye-Barker Fire & Safety (largest fire protection provider in US) acquires Sound & Communications Systems, Inc. to expand services.
data.description: In Atlanta on October 17, 2025, Pye-Barker Fire & Safety, the leading provider of fire protection, life safety, and security services in the US, announced the acquisition of Sound & Communications Systems, Inc., a company specializing in security and fire alarm services. This acquisition marks a significant expansion for Pye-Barker Fire & Safety, positioning them as a major player in the industry.

data.title: NYSE reports automakers are seeking 5-year extension for tariff relief; Pre-market update by Kristen Scholer on October 17, 2025.
data.description: On October 17, 2025, the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) released its daily pre-market update live from the NYSE Trading Floor. Investors can gain valuable market insights before the trading session commences by accessing the NYSE Pre-market update. Kristen Scholer presented the update on October 17, informing viewers about the latest trends and figures to guide their trading decisions. This real-time information from the NYSE Trading Floor is essential for understanding market conditions and making informed investment choices.

data.title: New calculator estimates college costs considering grants and scholarships. 20+ private colleges offer the tool.
data.description: Around 20 private colleges have introduced a new online tool that calculates students' actual expenses by considering need-based grants and scholarships.

data.title: Illumynt's Q3 2025 update: Revenue exceeds $30 million, new CEO appointed, 3 large-scale data center projects executed successfully.
data.description: In the third quarter of 2025, in Burlington, Mass., illumynt experienced a period of significant activity. The company announced a new CEO and successfully implemented various initiatives to drive organizational growth. Revenues for the quarter surpassed $30 million, aligning with the company's 2025 financial goals. Particularly noteworthy was the completion of three major data center decommission projects, with minimal waste generated. Notably, all artificial intelligence-focused enterprise equipment and components were effectively repurposed.

data.title: Keysight acquires Optical Solutions from Synopsys and PowerArtist from Ansys to enhance design capabilities. #KEYS #NYSE #NASDAQ
data.description: Keysight Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: KEYS) based in Santa Rosa, California, recently finalized acquisitions that further enhance its offerings. The company acquired the Optical Solutions Group from Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS) and PowerArtist from Ansys, Inc. This move bolsters Keysight's design engineering software portfolio and computer-aided engineering capabilities, facilitating a quicker route to market for customers' innovative designs. The Optical Solutions Group, known for its optical design and analysis expertise, adds valuable capabilities to Keysight's arsenal.

data.title: The Arena Group acquires ShopHQ, former $500 million revenue company, focusing on E-Commerce and interactive selling.
data.description: In New York, The Arena Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE American: AREN) has successfully obtained the intellectual property of ShopHQ from IV Media, LLC. The Arena Group aims to enhance the value of the 35-year-old brand, primarily emphasizing E-Commerce and interactive selling. By acquiring ShopHQ, the Company now has access to valuable first-party customer data. ShopHQ was previously a company generating over $500 million in revenue. Arena plans to leverage this data alongside its expansive reach to millions of customers.

data.title: Butterfly Network to announce Q3 2025 financial results on Oct 31; CEO and CFO to host call at 8 am.
data.description: Butterfly Network, a digital health company (NYSE: BFLY), will be releasing its third-quarter 2025 financial results on October 31, 2025, before the market opens. The conference call and webcast, hosted by Joseph DeVivo, President, Chief Executive Officer, and Chairman of the Board, and Megan Carlson, Interim Chief Financial Officer, will take place at 8:00 a.m. (EST). Butterfly is known for revolutionizing healthcare with handheld, whole-body ultrasound technology and user-friendly software. Investors and stakeholders are eagerly awaiting the financial updates.

data.title: Greece's data center market grows, Athens houses future centers with over 250 MW capacity, 5x more than current. New players boost investor interest.
data.description: A database titled "Greece Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" has been newly available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. Currently, Athens houses all of Greece's upcoming data centers, boasting over 250 MW of total IT power capacity. The upcoming data center capacity in the country is nearly five times larger than the existing capacity. This growth is attracting new players like Edgenex and Apto & Dromeus Capital, indicating a surge in investor interest and market diversification. Key industry players such as Digital Realty are also involved in the expanding data center landscape.

data.title: Samsung Electronics acquires Xealth, a digital health platform, aiming to advance connected care vision.
data.description: In a recent announcement from Seoul, South Korea, and Seattle, Samsung Electronics has successfully completed the acquisition of Xealth, a prominent digital health platform. This strategic move aims to propel Samsung's vision for connected care.

data.title: ClearNote Health to present advancements in cancer detection at ESMO Congress 2025 in San Diego with focus on 5hmC.
data.description: In San Diego, ClearNote Health revealed plans to deliver a series of presentations at the European Society for Medical Oncology Congress in 2025. The number of presentations and specific topics were not disclosed in the announcement. ClearNote Health's participation underscores its commitment to showcasing advancements in medical oncology research and innovation at this prominent global event.
